But don't expect a magic cure. Life's tough, and you gotta be tougher.

It's a big decision. Weigh the pros and cons carefully and make sure it's the right choice for you.

Is it okay to use AI tools for creative projects or academic work?

Well, it depends.

AI tools can be handy for brainstorming or getting past a block, kinda like having an extra set of hands. They can spit out ideas or help you organize your thoughts. But they ain't gonna do the work for you.

Same goes for academics. If you're using AI to help with research or writing, cite it properly. Don't try to fool your professor into thinking it's all your own work.

Bottom line: AI can be a tool, but it ain't a replacement for hard work and integrity.

My child is being bullied at school. What's the best way to handle this?

Bullying's a tough situation.  First, get all the facts. Talk to your child, calmly, about what's happening.  Who, what, when, where - get specifics. Then, talk to the school.  Don't go in angry, just state the facts and ask what they're doing to address it.  

Document everything: dates, times, witnesses, any communication with the school.  Sometimes, you gotta be persistent. If the school isn't taking it seriously, consider going up the chain of command or involving other parents.

Teach your child some basic self-defense, not necessarily physical, but how to stand up for themselves verbally and walk away. Confidence can be a deterrent.  

And keep an eye on your child. Make sure they know they can come to you with anything.
